ha._comment: Optional - remove this block if you prefer the setup screen or want per-device localStorage auth

widgets.text: A browser-based dashboard for Home Assistant
Pixel-perfect fixed-canvas layouts driven entirely by JSON config files.
No addons. No automations. Just drop files into your HA www folder.
Works on tablets, iPads, smart TVs, and any device with a browser.
Use the dots below to navigate between pages
Labels display text and can bind to any HA entity. Values update in real time via WebSocket.
Built-in formats: power, kwh, percent  -  auto-switch from W to kW at 1000
State-based color  -  color changes based on entity value threshold
Rect is a plain colored rectangle. Used for panel backgrounds, dividers, and decorative elements.
Any theme token or hex color as background:
Thin rects make useful dividers and accents
Bar shows a numeric entity value as a horizontal fill. Track background is built in - no extra rect needed.
Optional: add "background" to override the default grey track. Thresholds color the fill.
{ "below": 33, "color": "#1196ee" }  ->  blue  when value is 0-33% of max
{ "below": 66, "color": "primary"  }  ->  green when value is 33-66% of max
{ "default": true, "color": "warning" }  ->  amber when value is 66-100% of max
Buttons reflect entity state visually and call a HA service on tap. Icons from Font Awesome 4.
entity       ->  which HA entity to watch for state changes
icon_on/off  ->  Font Awesome unicode codepoint e.g. "[fa-lightbulb-o]" for lightbulb
states       ->  background, icon_color, label_color per entity state
action       ->  service to call and entity_id to target on tap
All state styling lives here in the config - no HA automations needed.
Clock displays current device time in HH:MM, updated every second. No HA entity needed.
Typical usage - small clock in a page header
Place on page 0 (coming soon) to show on every page without repeating in config.
Uses local device time - works even when HA connection is lost.
Supports any font_size - from tiny status bar clock to full-screen display.
These two widgets watch sensor.solar_power - one shows above 0, one shows below 0:
SVG arc gauges driven by HA entities. Thresholds change color by percentage of range.

Solar power
start: 180  end: 360 (half arc)
icon in label
thresholds    ->  same as bar widget, color by % of range

All state styling lives in the config.
No HA automations needed.
attr.xxx templates in label text, state overrides for hvac mode icon, attribute overrides for hvac_action colour.
